commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer approval of the new Discord TTL policy plus external-PR proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff does not add dependencies, secrets, or new permissions, but it intentionally widens the default stale-callback action window and needs explicit maintainer acceptance.; Review finding: [P3] Remove the release-owned changelog entry — CHANGELOG.md:28 (sha=17ef37cab6ba4e5bc32f1702f696d188f506bb1c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Discord component callback fallback TTL from 30 minutes to 7 days, raises the `agentComponents.ttlMs` maximum from 24 hours to 30 days, and updates related docs, metadata, and tests.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +21, Docs +1, Generated 0. Total +22 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the current behavior: source inspection shows current main uses a 30-minute fallback TTL and 24-hour config cap. I did not run a live multi-hour Discord interaction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer approval of the new Discord TTL policy plus external-PR proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(discord): extend component callback ttl
target.body:
## Summary

- Raise the default Discord component callback registry TTL from 30 minutes to 7 days.
- Expand the bounded `agentComponents.ttlMs` override maximum from 24 hours to 30 days.
- Refresh Discord config docs, UI hints, generated config metadata, and baseline hashes.
- Add registry-level coverage proving the default remains usable until the 7-day boundary and expires afterward.

This keeps asynchronous review and approval cards usable by default while preserving an explicit bounded override for deployments that need a different lifetime. Intentionally out of scope: changing component authorization, reusable-component semantics, or Discord interaction handling outside registry TTLs.

Reviewers should focus on the default/max TTL choice and whether the docs describe the stale-callback tradeoff clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #90390

Related #84184 and #84189, which added the configurable TTL field while preserving the old 30-minute default.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord component callbacks without an explicit `agentComponents.ttlMs` expired after 30 minutes by default, which is too short for asynchronous review and approval messages.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw source checkout with Discord registry/config/send tests and generated config metadata checks; no live Discord credentials were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/components.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/send.components.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
  - `pnpm lint:extensions`
  - `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers`
  - `pnpm check:changelog-attributions`
  - `pnpm lint:extensions:no-guarded-wildcard-reexports`
  - `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports`
  - `pnpm dup:check:coverage`
  - `pnpm deps:pins:check`
  - `pnpm deps:patches:check`
  - `pnpm check:media-download-helpers`
  - `pnpm check:runtime-sidecar-loaders`
  - `pnpm check:import-cycles`
  - `pnpm lint:webhook:no-low-level-body-read`
  - `pnpm lint:auth:no-pairing-store-group`
  - `pnpm lint:auth:pairing-account-scope`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix: `components.test.ts` now registers a component without `ttlMs`, verifies it resolves just before 7 days, and verifies it expires just after the 7-day boundary. Config schema tests accept the 30-day max and reject max+1.
- Observed result after fix: all listed commands passed.
- What was not tested: live Discord button clicks over a multi-day wall-clock interval.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live interval is impractical for a small PR; the registry clock-boundary test covers the expiration logic deterministically, and send-path tests continue to cover configured TTL propagation.
- Before evidence: current main defined `DEFAULT_COMPONENT_TTL_MS = 30 * 60 * 1000`, documented default `1800000`, and capped `agentComponents.ttlMs` at `86400000`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run are listed in the proof section above.

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Added a default registry TTL boundary test in `extensions/discord/src/components.test.ts`.
- Updated Discord config schema tests for the new 30-day maximum and max+1 rejection.
- Regenerated channel config metadata and config docs baseline hash.

`pnpm check:changed` itself could not be used directly in this environment because the remote Testbox wrapper failed its Crabbox sanity check, and local child mode requires a `corepack` binary that was not available on PATH. I ran the changed-lane typecheck, lint, and guard commands directly instead.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

Highest-risk area: longer-lived Discord component callbacks can keep old messages actionable for longer.

Mitigation: `allowedUsers` and component authorization semantics are unchanged; deployments can lower `agentComponents.ttlMs`; the schema still enforces a finite maximum, now 30 days.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI.

Still waiting on: maintainer decision on the 7-day default and 30-day maximum.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
